description: Functions to interact with `goose_selection_vector`. Selection vectors define a selection on top of a vector. Lets say that a filter filters out all `VARCHAR`-rows containing `hello`. Then, instead of creating a full new copy of the filtered-out data, it is possible to use a selection vector only selecting the rows satisfying the filter.

comment.description:
Creates a new selection vector of size `size`.
Must be destroyed with `goose_destroy_selection_vector`.

param_comments.size: The size of the selection vector.

comment.description: Destroys the selection vector and de-allocates its memory.
Access the data pointer of a selection vector.
